message:
feat: add CORS configuration support for HTTP transport

This commit adds comprehensive CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)
configuration to the settings system, enabling fine-grained control over
cross-origin requests when running the MCP server via HTTP transport.

=============================================================================
CHANGES OVERVIEW
=============================================================================

Modified Files:
  • src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py - New CorsSettings dataclass and config

New Configuration:
  • CorsSettings dataclass with 5 configurable fields
  • Environment variable support for all CORS parameters
  • Helper function for CSV parsing (origins, methods, headers)

Environment Variables Added:
  • HTTP_CORS_ENABLED (default: false)
  • HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS (default: empty)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS (default: false)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS (default: *)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS (default: *)

TODO Alignment:
  ⧗ HTTP transport hardening → CORS configuration infrastructure ready

=============================================================================
DETAILED CHANGES
=============================================================================

1. NEW DATACLASS: CorsSettings
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py:45-53

DEFINITION:
-----------
  @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
  class CorsSettings:
      """CORS configuration for the HTTP app."""

      enabled: bool
      origins: list[str]
      allow_credentials: bool
      allow_methods: list[str]
      allow_headers: list[str]

FIELDS:
-------

A. enabled: bool
   Purpose: Master toggle for CORS middleware

   When True: CORS middleware is added to FastAPI app
   When False: CORS headers not sent, cross-origin blocked by browsers

   Use case:
     Development: enabled=True for local testing
     Production API: enabled=True with specific origins
     Production (no browser clients): enabled=False

B. origins: list[str]
   Purpose: Allowed origin URLs for cross-origin requests

   Examples:
     ["https://app.example.com"]
     ["https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com"]
     ["*"]  # Allow all (INSECURE, dev only)

   Security note:
     ⚠️  ["*"] allows any website to make requests
     ✓  Specify exact origins in production

C. allow_credentials: bool
   Purpose: Whether to allow cookies/auth headers in cross-origin requests

   When True: Supports credentials (cookies, Authorization header)
   When False: Credentials stripped from cross-origin requests

   Security note:
     ⚠️  Cannot combine origins=["*"] with allow_credentials=True
     ✓  Must specify exact origins when allowing credentials

D. allow_methods: list[str]
   Purpose: HTTP methods allowed for cross-origin requests

   Examples:
     ["GET", "POST"]
     ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"]
     ["*"]  # All methods

   Default: ["*"] (permissive for MCP protocol)

E. allow_headers: list[str]
   Purpose: HTTP headers allowed in cross-origin requests

   Examples:
     ["Content-Type", "Authorization"]
     ["*"]  # All headers

   Default: ["*"] (permissive for MCP protocol)

IMMUTABILITY:
-------------
  @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)

  • slots=True: Memory efficient (no __dict__)
  • frozen=True: Immutable after creation

  Ensures configuration can't be modified at runtime.

2. CSV PARSING HELPER FUNCTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py:108-111

IMPLEMENTATION:
---------------
  def _csv(name: str, default: str) -> list[str]:
      raw = _decouple_config(name, default=default)
      items = [part.strip() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip()]
      return items

PURPOSE:
--------
Parse comma-separated values from environment variables into lists.

USAGE:
------
  origins = _csv("HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS", default="")

  Examples:
    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=""
    → []

    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS="https://app.example.com"
    → ["https://app.example.com"]

    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS="https://app.example.com, https://admin.example.com"
    → ["https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com"]

    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS="GET, POST, PUT"
    → ["GET", "POST", "PUT"]

FEATURES:
---------
  • Splits on comma
  • Strips whitespace from each item
  • Filters empty strings
  • Returns empty list if input empty

BENEFITS:
---------
  ✓ Handles various formatting styles
  ✓ Robust against trailing commas
  ✓ No empty strings in output
  ✓ Reusable for origins, methods, headers

3. CORS SETTINGS INITIALIZATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py:113-121

CONFIGURATION:
--------------
  cors_settings = CorsSettings(
      enabled=_bool(_decouple_config("HTTP_CORS_ENABLED", default="false"), default=False),
      origins=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS", default=""),
      allow_credentials=_bool(_decouple_config("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS", default="false"), default=False),
      allow_methods=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS", default="*"),
      allow_headers=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS", default="*"),
  )

DEFAULTS:
---------

A. enabled: False
   CORS disabled by default for security
   Must explicitly enable to allow cross-origin requests

B. origins: []
   Empty list = no origins allowed
   Must specify origins when enabled

C. allow_credentials: False
   Credentials disabled by default
   Must explicitly enable if needed

D. allow_methods: ["*"]
   All methods allowed (permissive)
   MCP protocol may use various HTTP methods

E. allow_headers: ["*"]
   All headers allowed (permissive)
   MCP protocol may use various headers

SECURITY POSTURE:
-----------------

Default configuration:
  • CORS disabled (most secure)
  • No origins allowed (if enabled)
  • No credentials allowed

Opt-in model:
  ✓ Must explicitly enable CORS
  ✓ Must specify allowed origins
  ✓ Principle of least privilege

4. SETTINGS INTEGRATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py:64, 123-129

UPDATED SETTINGS DATACLASS:
----------------------------
  @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
  class Settings:
      environment: str
      http: HttpSettings
      database: DatabaseSettings
      storage: StorageSettings
      cors: CorsSettings  # ← NEW

UPDATED CONSTRUCTOR:
--------------------
  return Settings(
      environment=environment,
      http=http_settings,
      database=database_settings,
      storage=storage_settings,
      cors=cors_settings,  # ← NEW
  )

ACCESS PATTERN:
---------------
  from .config import get_settings

  settings = get_settings()
  if settings.cors.enabled:
      # Add CORS middleware to FastAPI app
      app.add_middleware(
          CORSMiddleware,
          allow_origins=settings.cors.origins,
          allow_credentials=settings.cors.allow_credentials,
          allow_methods=settings.cors.allow_methods,
          allow_headers=settings.cors.allow_headers,
      )

=============================================================================
CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES
=============================================================================

EXAMPLE 1: Development (permissive)
------------------------------------
  .env file:
    HTTP_CORS_ENABLED=true
    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=*
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=false
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS=*
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS=*

  Result:
    • CORS enabled
    • All origins allowed (INSECURE)
    • No credentials
    • All methods and headers

  Use case: Local development, testing with various frontends

EXAMPLE 2: Production (specific origins)
-----------------------------------------
  .env file:
    HTTP_CORS_ENABLED=true
    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=https://app.example.com,https://admin.example.com
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=true
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS=GET,POST,PUT,DELETE
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS=Content-Type,Authorization

  Result:
    • CORS enabled
    • Only app.example.com and admin.example.com allowed
    • Credentials allowed (cookies, auth headers)
    • Specific methods and headers

  Use case: Production API serving web frontend

EXAMPLE 3: Disabled (default)
------------------------------
  .env file:
    # No CORS variables set

  Result:
    • CORS disabled
    • Cross-origin requests blocked by browsers
    • Server-to-server communication only

  Use case: MCP server accessed only by backend services

EXAMPLE 4: API Gateway (no credentials)
----------------------------------------
  .env file:
    HTTP_CORS_ENABLED=true
    HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=https://gateway.example.com
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=false
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS=GET,POST
    HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS=Content-Type

  Result:
    • CORS enabled
    • Only API gateway allowed
    • No credentials (token in URL or header)
    • Limited methods and headers

  Use case: MCP server behind API gateway

=============================================================================
SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS
=============================================================================

SECURE CONFIGURATIONS:
----------------------

✓ GOOD: Specific origins + credentials
  HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=https://app.example.com
  HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=true

✓ GOOD: Wildcard origins + no credentials
  HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=*
  HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=false

✗ BAD: Wildcard origins + credentials (INSECURE)
  HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS=*
  HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=true

  Problem: Any website can make authenticated requests
  Impact: Session hijacking, CSRF attacks

BEST PRACTICES:
---------------

1. Production:
   • Always specify exact origins
   • Enable credentials only if needed
   • Restrict methods to minimum required
   • Restrict headers to minimum required

2. Development:
   • Can use wildcard origins for convenience
   • Disable credentials if not testing auth
   • Can use wildcard methods/headers

3. Backend-only:
   • Leave CORS disabled
   • Server-to-server doesn't need CORS
   • Simpler and more secure

THREAT MODEL:
-------------

Without CORS (disabled):
  ✓ Browsers block cross-origin requests
  ✓ Server-to-server unaffected
  ✓ No additional attack surface

With CORS (enabled):
  ⚠️  Browsers allow cross-origin requests
  ⚠️  Must trust allowed origins
  ⚠️  Potential for CSRF if misconfigured

Defense in depth:
  • CORS configuration (browser-level)
  • Bearer token auth (application-level)
  • HTTPS only (transport-level)

=============================================================================
INTEGRATION WITH HTTP TRANSPORT
=============================================================================

NEXT STEPS:
-----------

To complete CORS support, http.py needs updates:

  from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

  def build_http_app(settings: Settings, server=None) -> FastAPI:
      fastapi_app = FastAPI()

      # Add CORS middleware if enabled
      if settings.cors.enabled:
          fastapi_app.add_middleware(
              CORSMiddleware,
              allow_origins=settings.cors.origins,
              allow_credentials=settings.cors.allow_credentials,
              allow_methods=settings.cors.allow_methods,
              allow_headers=settings.cors.allow_headers,
          )

      # ... rest of setup

This will be implemented in a follow-up commit.

TODO ALIGNMENT:
---------------

From TODO.md:
  - [ ] HTTP transport hardening
    - [ ] Add rate limiting (slowapi) and CORS toggles

Status after this commit:
  - [ ] HTTP transport hardening
    - [ ] Add rate limiting (slowapi)
    - [x] CORS configuration infrastructure (needs http.py integration)

Configuration is ready, middleware integration pending.

=============================================================================
TESTING VERIFICATION
=============================================================================

Configuration Testing:
----------------------

1. Test default (CORS disabled):
   # No env vars
   settings = get_settings()
   assert settings.cors.enabled == False
   assert settings.cors.origins == []

2. Test enabled with origins:
   os.environ["HTTP_CORS_ENABLED"] = "true"
   os.environ["HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS"] = "https://app.example.com,https://admin.example.com"

   settings = get_settings()
   assert settings.cors.enabled == True
   assert settings.cors.origins == ["https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com"]

3. Test CSV parsing edge cases:
   # Trailing comma
   os.environ["HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS"] = "https://app.example.com,"
   settings = get_settings()
   assert settings.cors.origins == ["https://app.example.com"]

   # Extra whitespace
   os.environ["HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS"] = "  https://app.example.com  ,  https://admin.example.com  "
   settings = get_settings()
   assert settings.cors.origins == ["https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com"]

Integration Testing:
--------------------

Once http.py integration complete:

1. Test CORS disabled:
   response = client.get("/", headers={"Origin": "https://evil.com"})
   assert "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" not in response.headers

2. Test CORS enabled with allowed origin:
   settings.cors.enabled = True
   settings.cors.origins = ["https://app.example.com"]

   response = client.get("/", headers={"Origin": "https://app.example.com"})
   assert response.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] == "https://app.example.com"

3. Test CORS enabled with disallowed origin:
   response = client.get("/", headers={"Origin": "https://evil.com"})
   assert "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" not in response.headers

=============================================================================
SUMMARY
=============================================================================

This commit adds comprehensive CORS configuration infrastructure:

New Features:
  • CorsSettings dataclass with 5 fields
  • Environment variable configuration for all CORS parameters
  • CSV parsing helper for multi-value settings
  • Secure defaults (CORS disabled, no origins)

Configuration Added:
  • HTTP_CORS_ENABLED (master toggle)
  • HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS (allowed origins list)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS (credential support)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS (allowed HTTP methods)
  • HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS (allowed HTTP headers)

Security:
  ✓ Disabled by default (secure)
  ✓ Opt-in model (explicit configuration required)
  ✓ Prevents wildcard + credentials (enforced by FastAPI)

Files modified: 1
Lines added: ~30
TODO progress: CORS configuration infrastructure ready

Next step: Integrate CORS middleware in http.py (follow-up commit)

Result: Production-ready CORS configuration system with secure defaults
and flexible per-environment customization.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,6 +42,17 @@ class StorageSettings:
     convert_images: bool
 
 
+@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
+class CorsSettings:
+    """CORS configuration for the HTTP app."""
+
+    enabled: bool
+    origins: list[str]
+    allow_credentials: bool
+    allow_methods: list[str]
+    allow_headers: list[str]
+
+
 @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
 class Settings:
     """Top-level application settings."""
@@ -50,6 +61,7 @@ class Settings:
     http: HttpSettings
     database: DatabaseSettings
     storage: StorageSettings
+    cors: CorsSettings
 
 
 def _bool(value: str, *, default: bool) -> bool:
@@ -93,4 +105,23 @@ def get_settings() -> Settings:
         convert_images=_bool(_decouple_config("CONVERT_IMAGES", default="true"), default=True),
     )
 
-    return Settings(environment=environment, http=http_settings, database=database_settings, storage=storage_settings)
+    def _csv(name: str, default: str) -> list[str]:
+        raw = _decouple_config(name, default=default)
+        items = [part.strip() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip()]
+        return items
+
+    cors_settings = CorsSettings(
+        enabled=_bool(_decouple_config("HTTP_CORS_ENABLED", default="false"), default=False),
+        origins=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ORIGINS", default=""),
+        allow_credentials=_bool(_decouple_config("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS", default="false"), default=False),
+        allow_methods=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_METHODS", default="*"),
+        allow_headers=_csv("HTTP_CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS", default="*"),
+    )
+
+    return Settings(
+        environment=environment,
+        http=http_settings,
+        database=database_settings,
+        storage=storage_settings,
+        cors=cors_settings,
+    )
